title: sv.messaging.send.input
description: Input for sv.messaging.send — a one-way message-delivery tool that places one message on the SINGLE SHARED THREAD identified by {caller} ∪ recipients. Every recipient sees the message on the same thread and sees the other recipients in the inbound envelope's `to` field. Supply exactly one of `recipients` or `scope`. The calling participant is auto-included in the thread's participant set — do not list yourself in `recipients`. Use sv.messaging.multicast when you want each recipient to receive the message on its own independent 1-1 thread. The platform delivers messages; it does NOT wait for or return the recipients' replies.

recipients.description: Explicit recipient list. Each entry is a canonical address (`agent:<32-hex>`, `unit:<32-hex>`, `human:<32-hex>`) of a participant in the caller's tenant. Connectors (`connector:<32-hex>`) are non-routable and are rejected with an UnroutableTarget error. Mutually exclusive with `scope`.

items.description: Canonical scheme:no-dash-hex address of a recipient.

scope.description: Resolve the recipient set from the caller's position in the member graph: 'unit-members' (valid for a unit caller) targets the members of the calling unit; 'siblings' targets the caller's siblings under their parent unit(s). The resolved set forms the shared thread's participants together with the caller. Mutually exclusive with `recipients`.

message.description: The message payload to deliver. Opaque to the platform; a JSON string or object.

reason.description: Optional rationale recorded verbatim on the MessageSent activity. Never the model's hidden chain-of-thought.
